schema_notes: Zoom-only design (no Jitsi fallback). Anchored to the messy schedule fixture written by install.sh into ~/clawbench-schedule.txt. The fixture has exactly 6 events, all dated in May 2026 with weekdays cross-checked (Wed 5/13, Fri 5/15, Mon 5/18, Tue 5/19, Thu 5/21, Fri 5/22). The agent must (a) parse the messy text into structured JSON, (b) self-configure ~/.cli-anything-zoom/{config,tokens}.json from env vars ZOOM_CLIENT_ID/ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET/ZOOM_REFRESH_TOKEN (install.sh deliberately wipes that dir after using the same credentials to reset the test account to zero meetings), then `zoom auth status` to verify, (c) call `zoom meeting create --topic ... --start-time ... --duration ...` for each of 6 events and embed the returned join_url in the corresponding VEVENT's LOCATION/DESCRIPTION, (d) emit a valid RFC-5545 ICS file with one VEVENT per event, (e) actually launch gnome-calendar (Linux GUI calendar, preinstalled by install.sh) with these events visible and capture a scrot screenshot of the calendar window, (f) write a narrative scheduling_report.md. install.sh fails fast if any of the three Zoom env vars are empty (no Jitsi fallback path).

events.evidence_in_fixture: "Q2 planning sync, Wed May 13 2026 at 14:00, 60 min, w/ Alice and Bob"
"Bug triage — Friday May 15, 10am, half an hour"
"Tech review of the new pipeline, May 18 (Mon) 3pm, 45 minutes"
"1:1 with manager, Tuesday next week (May 19) 11:30, 30 min"
"Customer demo (acme corp), Thursday May 21 16:00, runs 1 hour"

ics_requirements.datetime_format_hint: floating local YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS (no Z, no TZID)
ics_requirements.rationale: Each VEVENT carries a Zoom join_url returned by `zoom meeting create`; the numeric meeting ID in the URL must be unique across the 6 events (one fresh Zoom meeting per event, no room reuse).

executor_self_config_steps: Read ZOOM_CLIENT_ID / ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET / ZOOM_REFRESH_TOKEN from env
Run `zoom auth status` to trigger refresh of access_token and validate
zoom.post_install_state: ~/.cli-anything-zoom/ wiped clean — executor must re-create from env vars

per_event_match_rules.start_time_basis: Zoom returns start_time in UTC; treat the ICS local-floating DTSTART as the same wall-clock moment in the executor's local timezone — supervisor can normalise via `date -d '<DTSTART local>' --utc` or just compare HH:MM if the test env runs UTC

supervisor_check.infra_error_fallback: If `zoom meeting list` errors at eval time (network, revoked token), drop the live verification (b) and grade only the ICS-side URL pattern (a) + the executor's own create-call evidence in trace; treat topic/start match as +0.05 bonus instead of hard requirement
supervisor_check.trace_evidence_required: Executor's trace MUST show self-configuration of ~/.cli-anything-zoom/{config,tokens}.json (file writes / `zoom auth setup` / equivalent) followed by a `zoom auth status` invocation; missing this evidence => 0.18 checkpoint scores 0 (cap 0.55)

calendar_screenshot.rationale: After parsing + ICS emit + Zoom create, the agent imports the events into gnome-calendar (or another running GUI calendar) under Xvfb and uses scrot to capture the calendar window. The screenshot proves an actual GUI calendar showed the events on screen — not just an ICS file on disk. We do not pixel-diff the screenshot; we only require valid PNG and minimum dimensions, plus a calendar-app invocation in the trace.

ground_truth.meeting_path_note: Zoom-only design. install.sh resets the test account to zero meetings before the task starts (using the same env-var credentials), then wipes ~/.cli-anything-zoom/. Executor must self-configure config + tokens from env vars and run `zoom auth status` to validate before any `zoom meeting create`. Each of 6 events corresponds to a single fresh Zoom meeting (one meeting id per event, all 6 distinct). Eval verifies via `zoom meeting list --json` against the test account post-execution: ≥6 meetings present, each ground-truth event matched by at least one Zoom meeting (topic keywords + start_time within ±5 min). install.sh fails fast (exit 1) if ZOOM_REFRESH_TOKEN is empty / invalid → run treated as infra_error.

conflict_check.rationale: User wants a sanity check on the scheduled events: no two should overlap (hard conflict), and same-day adjacent events should have at least 60min gap (near-conflict). Logic: hard_conflicts = pairs (i,j) where start_i < end_j AND start_j < end_i; near_conflicts = same-day consecutive pairs sorted by start where (next.start - prev.end).total_minutes is in [0, 60). For the fixture written by install.sh — 6 events on 6 distinct weekdays in May 2026 — both counts are 0 by construction.
conflict_check.data_field_required: conflict_check object in parsed_events.json with hard_conflicts (or events_with_overlap) array AND near_conflicts (or near_conflict_pairs) array
conflict_check.tolerance: Both arrays must be present and non-fabricated. For this fixture both must be empty (or equivalent zero-count). A non-zero count would indicate the executor's conflict logic is broken — the fixture is conflict-free by construction (verified across the 6 weekday-distinct dates 5/13 Wed 14:00+60, 5/15 Fri 10:00+30, 5/18 Mon 15:00+45, 5/19 Tue 11:30+30, 5/21 Thu 16:00+60, 5/22 Fri 16:00+45).
